name: AWS WAF Restricted Content-Type Enforcement for API Endpoints
description: Enforces an allowlist of expected Content-Type headers (default: application/json) on state-changing API routes to reduce content-type confusion and parser mismatch attack surface.
objective: Allow only expected Content-Type values for write operations on API endpoints, while permitting safe methods to bypass enforcement when desired.
risk_mitigated: Content-Type confusion attacks against API parsers
Unexpected payload formats reaching deserialization and business logic
Evasion attempts that rely on alternate media types
deployment_notes: Deploy in COUNT mode first for 7-14 days and review sampled requests and WAF logs for legitimate clients with missing or variant Content-Type headers.
After tuning allowlists and exclusions, switch to BLOCK mode for targeted API paths.
Roll out per service or path prefix (/api/v1, /api/v2) rather than globally when API behaviors differ.
tuning_notes: Scope rule to state-changing methods (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) and optionally exclude GET/HEAD from evaluation.
If file upload endpoints exist, add route-specific exceptions for multipart/form-data and validate boundary handling in upstream services.
If some endpoints legitimately use application/x-www-form-urlencoded or vendor media types (e.g., application/vnd.api+json), include exact allowed values.
Consider normalizing or matching only media type token (before semicolon) if clients send charset parameters.

alerts: Spike in blocked requests for a newly protected API route
Sustained count hits after initial tuning window

implementation_notes: Default action is COUNT for safe rollout; change to block after validation.
Add additional allowed content-types as extra statements in the inner or_statement.
For upload endpoints, add higher-priority allow rules scoped to path + method + multipart/form-data before this rule.
